name: Hurricane

spec.method_detail: Shake with ice. Strain over crushed ice in hurricane glass.

history: Pat O'Brien's Bar in New Orleans created the Hurricane as a way to use up surplus rum (whiskey was scarce during World War II). The name comes from the hurricane lamp-shaped glass. It became the iconic Bourbon Street tourist drink. The Pat O'Brien's version is much sweeter and simpler than the original recipe — a mix of passion fruit and citrus that's been bastardized into a sugar bomb in most tourist traps.
